institution.en: He's been in an institution since the crash.

plague.en: Ten Biblical plagues over Egypt, ranging from locusts to the death of the crown prince, finally forced Pharaoh to let Moses's people go.

psychological.en: I'm concerned about their psychological well-being.

shade.en: The old oak tree gave shade in the heat of the day.

persistent.en: She has had a persistent cough for weeks.

tolerance.en: Our customers can generally accept ten times the tolerance which we can achieve in our machining operations.

senior.en: He was four years her senior.

contemporary.en: Cervantes was a contemporary of Shakespeare.

content.en: You can't have any more - you'll have to content yourself with what you already have.

overweight.en: Apple common stock is one of our overweights.

mainstream.en: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.

cheek.en: You’ve got some cheek, asking me for money!

import.en: It was a matter of great import.

fiction.en: I am a great reader of fiction.

upbringing.en: Through her upbringing, Mildred's father had carefully programmed her to expect very little from life.

preserve.en: Every people have the right to preserve its identity and culture.

advocate.en: Since she started working with her advocate, she has become much more confident.

dust.en: to raise, or kick up, a dust

track.en: Can you see any tracks in the snow?

session.en: "Are we having a recording session?" / "Yes. We've even got some session musicians to provide some brass."

exhaust.en: Moisture of the earth is exhausted by evaporation.

praise.en: Be sure to praise Bobby for his excellent work at school this week.

relative.en: Why do my relatives always talk about sex?

breakthrough.en: Albert Einstein is credited with making some of the greatest breakthroughs in modern physics.

scratch.en: Her skin was covered with tiny scratches.

harmful.en: Wear a hat to protect your skin from harmful sunlight.

undergo.en: The project is undergoing great changes.

vital.en: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

fortunately.en: Fortunately, Sam's pet hamster was not dead.

commencement.en: The time of Henry VII nearly coincides with the commencement of what is termed modern history.

fetch.en: If you put some new tyres on it, and clean it up a bit, the car should fetch about $5,000

clumsy.en: He's very clumsy. I wouldn't trust him with carrying the dishes.

establishment.en: Since their establishment of the company in 1984, they have grown into a global business.

gallery.en: a clip-art gallery in a wordprocessor

desert.en: They were marooned on a desert island in the Pacific.

entitle.en: A passport entitles the bearer to travel to other countries.

relieve.en: I was greatly relieved by the jury's verdict.

colleague.en: Young Fortinbras,/ Holding a weak supposal of our worth/...Colleagued with the dream of his advantage,/...hath not failed to pester us with message/ Importing the surrender of those lands/Lost by his father. - Hamlet (Act I, Scene 2)

undertake.en: I hate people who try and undertake on the motorway.

convenient.en: Fast food might be convenient, but it's also very unhealthy.

column.en: It was too hard to read the text across the whole page, so I split it into two columns.

affectionate.en: She eulogised her always warm and affectionate brother.

regular.en: Bartenders usually know their regulars by name.

hinder.en: A drought hinders the growth of plants.

selection.en: The large number of good candidates made selection difficult.

tide.en: The doctor's no good this tide.
